### Post by Matthijs on 2007-04-17
Hi there....

Some app's like xmms have some strange hard readable font... I have search a lot, but did not find anything like it on the net. My thoughts are that there is something wrong with my xorg.conf.

I have a picture hosted so that maby you people can see what my problem is.
[Screenshot.png]("http://matthijs.rukker.org/images/Screenshot.png")

also here is my xorg.conf 
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:55:20 PST 2006

    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "MX Rev"
#    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"

    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier        "MX Rev"
    Driver            "evdev"
    Option            "Device" "/dev/input/event9"
    Option            "Protocol" "auto"
    Option            "CorePointer"
EndSection

#Section "InputDevice"
#    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
#    Driver         "mouse"
#    Option         "CorePointer"
#    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
#    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
#    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
#    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
#EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Generic Monitor"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 51.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Generic Video Card"
    Driver         "nvidia"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Generic Video Card"
    Monitor        "Generic Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "1400x1050_60 +0+0; 800x600 +0+0; 640x480 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
       Depth       24

EndSection

```

any help would be appreciated!! :)

---

### Post by NoNo_231 on 2007-04-17
Try changing the /usr/share/X11/fonts /usr/share/fonts/X11. First make a backup of your xorg.conf. After the change reload your xserver of reboot. In 6.10 the fonts moved the latter dir.

It should be like this:
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
....

i hope it will help.

---

### Post by Matthijs on 2007-04-17
Thank you, that did the trick!!
i am very happy now. 
First when i removed the 
```
    Option         "metamodes" "1400x1050_60 +0+0; 800x600 +0+0; 640x480 +0+0"
```
the fonts were alright then. but then the standard resolution wasn't right. now both work!

Thanks for your help!

---

### Post by RedSquirrel on 2007-04-17
> **NoNo_231 said:**
> Try changing the /usr/share/X11/fonts /usr/share/fonts/X11. First make a backup of your xorg.conf. After the change reload your xserver of reboot. In 6.10 the fonts moved the latter dir.

It should be like this:
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
....

i hope it will help.


Another possibility: rather than making the changes to /etc/X11/xorg.conf, one could create a symbolic link:

```
sudo ln -s  /usr/share/fonts/X11  /usr/share/X11/fonts
```

---
